title: Podman Containers lose network connectivity after some time
body:
### Issue Description

I have basically the same issue as the one described [#18504](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/18504), which was closed without solution, unfortunately: a podman container installed with Ansible cannot scrape its target after some time because the outbound connectivity does not work. A restart or a  podman system reset  temporarily fix the problem.
Executing `podman unshare --rootless-netns telnet $target $port` shows the same timeout issue.
The pasta driver is not available and the slirp4netns process is up.




### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. install a prometheus container using Ansible
2. wait some time
3. prometheus cannot scrape the the targets (all outbound connections timeout)
4. podman restart fixes the issue for several hours
5. podman reset + container recreation fixes the issue for about one week


### Describe the results you received

Connection timeout

### Describe the results you expected

The container should be able to connect to other hosts.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

### Additional information

Ansible task:
```
    - name: deploy
      containers.podman.podman_container:
        env:
        command: "--config.file=/prometheus/prometheus.yml --storage.tsdb.path=/prom_data --storage.tsdb.retention={{ settings[ansible_hostname[0:5]].RETENTION_TIME }}"
        name: prometheus
        image: prom/prometheus:v2.53.1
        userns: "keep-id"
        user: "2500:2500"
        network: bridge
        ports:
          - "9090:9090"
        log_driver: json-file
        log_opt:
          max_size: 50m
        state: started
        recreate: yes
        restart: yes
        volumes:
          - "{{ settings[ansible_hostname[0:5]].CFG_PATH }}:/prometheus:Z"
          - "{{ settings[ansible_hostname[0:5]].DATA_PATH }}:/prom_data:Z"
```

comments.body: We only support the latest version upstream, so unless you can reproduce the problem on podman 5.6 with the latest pasta version there isn't anything we can do upstream.

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@uj-ff48 Did you ever solve your disappearing network connections? I'm running the same version of podman as you on Redhat 8 and have a very similar problem with apps inside containers losing their connections to SQL databases on other hosts.
I'm not sure even where to look to troubleshoot this!

Hi Steve,
Apparently, creating the container in a different network (a custom one defined by me) fixes this issue. I have no idea why.
I hope this will solve your issue.
